sanskrit: यो मां पश्यति सर्वत्र सर्वं च मयि पश्यति । तस्याहं न प्रणश्यामि स च मे न प्रणश्यति ॥
hindiTranslation: जो सबमें मुझे देखता है और सबको मुझमें देखता है, उसके लिये मैं अदृश्य नहीं होता और वह मेरे लिये अदृश्य नहीं होता ॥
englishTranslation: Who sees Me in all beings and sees all beings in Me, I am never lost to that person, and that person is never lost to Me.
essence: Seeing the divine everywhere ends separation.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Seeing the divine in every being creates a living connection that cannot be broken. The one who sees this way is never separated from Krishna, and Krishna is never separated from that person.
simpleInsight: You do not have to force closeness when you learn to recognize the same presence everywhere.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield, after teaching steady meditation, Krishna shows Arjuna the fruit of true vision: a mind that no longer divides the world into separate pieces. The yogi who sees Krishna in all beings and all beings in Krishna lives in unbroken nearness.
modernRelevance: You walk into a hospital room and see the same fear in the patient, the nurse, and yourself. In that moment, the wall between 'me' and 'them' softens, and compassion becomes natural.
translationLiteral: यो (who) / माम् (Me) / पश्यति (sees) / सर्वत्र (everywhere) / सर्वम् (all) / च (and) / मयि (in Me) / पश्यति (sees) / तस्य (to that one) / अहम् (I) / न (not) / प्रणश्यामि (am lost) / सः (that one) / च (and) / मे (to Me) / न (not) / प्रणश्यति (is lost)
insight: It shifts perception from separation to shared presence, making devotion feel immediate rather than abstract.

quoteVariants.text: See Me in all beings. I never disappear from you.
All is in Me. I am in all.
I am not hidden from the one who sees all in Me.
See Me everywhere; never lose Me.
